speaker: AMIENS
text: Under the greenwood tree

speaker: AMIENS
text: Who loves to lie with me

speaker: AMIENS
text: And turn his merry note

speaker: AMIENS
text: Unto the sweet bird’s throat,

speaker: AMIENS
text: Come hither, come hither, come hither.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Here shall he see

speaker: AMIENS
text: No enemy

speaker: AMIENS
text: But winter and rough weather.

speaker: JAQUES
text: More, more, I prithee, more.

speaker: AMIENS
text: It will make you melancholy, Monsieur

speaker: AMIENS
text: Jaques.

speaker: JAQUES
text: I thank it. More, I prithee, more. I can suck

speaker: JAQUES
text: melancholy out of a song as a weasel sucks eggs.

speaker: JAQUES
text: More, I prithee, more.

speaker: AMIENS
text: My voice is ragged. I know I cannot please you.

speaker: JAQUES
text: I do not desire you to please me. I do desire

speaker: JAQUES
text: you to sing. Come, more, another stanzo. Call you

speaker: JAQUES
text: ’em “stanzos”?

speaker: AMIENS
text: What you will, Monsieur Jaques.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Nay, I care not for their names. They owe me

speaker: JAQUES
text: nothing. Will you sing?

speaker: AMIENS
text: More at your request than to please myself.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Well then, if ever I thank any man, I’ll thank

speaker: JAQUES
text: you. But that they call “compliment” is like th’

speaker: JAQUES
text: encounter of two dog-apes. And when a man thanks

speaker: JAQUES
text: me heartily, methinks I have given him a penny and

speaker: JAQUES
text: he renders me the beggarly thanks. Come, sing. And

speaker: JAQUES
text: you that will not, hold your tongues.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Well, I’ll end the song.—Sirs, cover the while;

speaker: AMIENS
text: the Duke will drink under this tree.—He hath been

speaker: AMIENS
text: all this day to look you.

speaker: JAQUES
text: And I have been all this day to avoid him. He is

speaker: JAQUES
text: too disputable for my company. I think of as many

speaker: JAQUES
text: matters as he, but I give heaven thanks and make no

speaker: JAQUES
text: boast of them. Come, warble, come.

speaker: ALL
text: Who doth ambition shun

speaker: ALL
text: And loves to live i’ th’ sun,

speaker: ALL
text: Seeking the food he eats

speaker: ALL
text: And pleased with what he gets,

speaker: ALL
text: Come hither, come hither, come hither.

speaker: ALL
text: Here shall he see

speaker: ALL
text: No enemy

speaker: ALL
text: But winter and rough weather.

speaker: JAQUES
text: I’ll give you a verse to this note that I made

speaker: JAQUES
text: yesterday in despite of my invention.

speaker: AMIENS
text: And I’ll sing it.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Thus it goes:

speaker: JAQUES
text: If it do come to pass

speaker: JAQUES
text: That any man turn ass,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Leaving his wealth and ease

speaker: JAQUES
text: A stubborn will to please,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Ducdame, ducdame, ducdame.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Here shall he see

speaker: JAQUES
text: Gross fools as he,

speaker: JAQUES
text: An if he will come to me.

speaker: AMIENS
text: What’s that “ducdame”?

speaker: JAQUES
text: ’Tis a Greek invocation to call fools into a

speaker: JAQUES
text: circle. I’ll go sleep if I can. If I cannot, I’ll rail

speaker: JAQUES
text: against all the first-born of Egypt.

speaker: AMIENS
text: And I’ll go seek the Duke. His banquet is

speaker: AMIENS
text: prepared.
